question.text: Under which section of the Income Tax Act can an investor claim tax deduction for investing in ELSS (Equity Linked Savings Scheme)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** ELSS investments qualify for tax deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, up to a maximum of Rs. 1.5 lakh per financial year. ELSS has the shortest lock-in period of 3 years among all Section 80C investments (PPF has 15 years, NSC has 5 years, tax-saving FD has 5 years). **The Trap:** Section 80D is for health insurance premiums. Section 10(23D) exempts the mutual fund scheme's own income from tax (not investor's income). Section 80E is for education loan interest. **Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in V-A and Series XXIV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ELSS is relevant for retired investors who still have taxable income. The 3-year lock-in is the shortest among 80C options, plus they get the potential for equity market returns.
